guideTitle: Build a Layered Application with Dependency Injection
guideSubhead: Learn the pattern behind a small data application: a domain model, a persistence layer hidden behind a repository, services that own the work, and one composition root that wires it all together.

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.subtitle: What layered architecture with dependency injection does at its core

chapters.title: Prerequisites
chapters.subtitle: The SDK, packages, and skills you need before you start

chapters.title: Quick Start Guide
chapters.subtitle: Create, wire, and run a working layered app in under ten minutes

chapters.title: High Level Architecture
chapters.subtitle: The layers, their boundaries, and how a request flows through them

chapters.title: Helpful Resources
chapters.subtitle: Documentation for the runtime, the ORM, and the DI container

chapters.title: The Domain Model
chapters.subtitle: The entity that flows through every layer, and the attributes it carries

chapters.title: The Database Context
chapters.subtitle: Mapping the entity to storage with an ORM context and a model configuration

chapters.title: The Repository Layer
chapters.subtitle: A narrow contract that hides the ORM from everything above it

chapters.title: The Import Service
chapters.subtitle: Reading external data, deserializing it, and turning it into entities

chapters.title: The Orchestration Service
chapters.subtitle: Composing the lower layers into one end-to-end use case

chapters.subtitle: Registering every contract, building the provider, and running the app

chapters.title: Extending the System
chapters.subtitle: Adding new entities, swapping implementations, and growing the layers
